title: cydonia cydonia var. maliformis
intro: Cydonia cydonia var. maliformis, also known as the medlar plant, is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the rose family and is native to Western Asia. The medlar plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. The health benefits of Cydonia cydonia var. maliformis include reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health, as well as its potential to exhibit antioxidant and anti-cancer properties. Herbal preparations of the medlar plant include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make medicinal teas, salves, and creams.
